skill.name: theme-factory
skill.description: Apply curated color palettes and font pairings to presentations, documents, reports, and HTML artifacts.
skill.summary: Apply professional visual themes to creative and business artifacts.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network, command execution, and high-risk keyword patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from Apache license URLs, Markdown inline code formatting, theme color hex values, and ordinary design text; no prompt injection or executable code was found.

low_findings.title: Apache License URLs Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The URLs appear only in the Apache 2.0 license text. They are reference links, not code that performs network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are standard Apache license references. No fetch, curl, wget, or other network execution appears near these locations.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The backticks wrap filenames, directories, and hex color values in Markdown. They are not Ruby or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every referenced location is Markdown text or inline code formatting. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Design Text Misclassified as Malware or Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The high-risk keyword matches occur in license text, theme descriptions, headings, and hex color values. No cryptographic function or C2 behavior exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review ties the flagged lines to prose and design tokens. The midnight-galaxy C2 hit is part of the color value #a490c2.

content.user_title: Apply Consistent Themes to Creative Artifacts
content.value_statement: Teams often spend time aligning colors, fonts, and presentation style by hand. This skill supplies reusable themes and applies them consistently to artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Presents ten named themes with defined colors and fonts.
Uses theme-showcase.pdf to help users compare visual options.
Applies selected palettes and typography to decks, documents, reports, and HTML artifacts.
Reads detailed theme specifications from the themes directory.
Creates a custom theme when preset options do not match the artifact.
limitations: It does not include code that edits files automatically.
Theme quality depends on the host tool applying the style correctly.
Available presets use a limited set of fonts and palettes.
It does not verify brand compliance against external guidelines.

use_cases.title: Polish a Client Presentation
use_cases.description: Apply a professional theme to slides before sharing a proposal or status update.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Reports
use_cases.description: Use one theme across recurring reports so teams can scan information quickly.

use_cases.title: Explore Brand Directions
use_cases.description: Compare preset themes or generate a custom direction for early visual exploration.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Preset Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the available themes and help me choose one for a short business presentation.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Chosen Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the Ocean Depths theme to my report and keep all headings, charts, and callouts consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Match an Audience
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend a theme for a healthcare executive deck, then explain the color and font choices before applying it.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Custom Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom theme for a premium sustainability brand, review it with me, then apply it across the artifact.

output_examples.input: Apply a clean theme to a startup pitch deck.
output_examples.output: The deck uses Modern Minimalist styling with charcoal headings, light gray dividers, and DejaVu Sans typography.

output_examples.input: Create a warm theme for a restaurant proposal.
output_examples.output: The proposal uses warm accent colors, readable body text, and consistent section headers across all pages.

output_examples.input: Help me compare themes for a product launch.
output: Tech Innovation fits a software launch with high contrast colors.
Ocean Depths fits a trusted enterprise message.
Sunset Boulevard fits a more energetic marketing launch.

best_practices: Review the theme showcase before selecting a final direction.
Confirm the audience and artifact type before applying a theme.
Check contrast, readability, and consistency after styling is applied.
anti_patterns: Do not apply a theme before the user confirms the choice.
Do not mix colors and fonts from many themes without a clear reason.
Do not use a decorative palette when the artifact needs dense information scanning.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps apply consistent color palettes and font pairings to presentations, documents, reports, and HTML artifacts.

faq.question: How many preset themes are included?
faq.answer: The skill includes ten preset themes, each with colors, fonts, and recommended use cases.

faq.question: Can it create a new theme?
faq.answer: Yes. It can generate a custom theme when the preset themes do not fit the artifact.

faq.question: Does it automatically edit files?
faq.answer: No. It gives styling instructions for the host assistant to apply within the current artifact workflow.

faq.question: Is the theme showcase required?
faq.answer: The showcase helps users compare options visually, but detailed theme files also describe each preset.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual review found no executable code, network behavior, prompt injection, or malicious intent in the skill files.
